content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

October 19, 1990

Ms. Marguerite Archie-Hudson  
P.O. Box 11656  
Los Angeles, CA 90011

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-608

Dear Ms. Archie-Hudson:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 You are a candidate for the 48th Assembly District.

Your letter, dated September 19, 1990, correctly summarizes my advice that it is permissible under the Political Reform Act for candidates for state and federal legislative offices to occupy the same physical premises during an election period, so long as each candidate pays, directly to the vendor, his or her own share of any costs related to his or her specific campaign.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor  
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
